title: Advanced File Handling Security Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of advanced security measures in modern file handling.

questions.question: What is a 'Path Traversal' attack in the context of file handling?
options: A way to automatically resize images.
When an attacker tries to access files and directories outside the intended folder by using sequences like '../'.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically generate a backup of the data.

questions.question: What's the best way to prevent 'Remote Code Execution' (RCE) from user-uploaded files?
options: By automatically renaming all files to a random string.
By using local disk storage only.
By ensuring your web server (like Nginx) is configured NOT to execute any files in your static assets folder.
By only using a CDN for serving assets.

questions.question: What is a 'File Bomb' or 'Zip Bomb' attack?
options: A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
A small, compressed file that consumes enormous amounts of memory or disk space when uncompressed, leading to DoS.
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Which Node.js method should you use to safely get just the file name part of a path string to prevent traversal attacks?
options: path.join()
path.basename()
path.dirname()
fs.readFileSync()

questions.question: Which open-source tool is commonly used to scan user-uploaded files for viruses and malware?
options: Postman
ClamAV
Redis
Sequelize
